channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Matlala Trial Continues as Court Reviews Attempted Murder Charges
item.description: The trial of Vusimusi 'Cat' Matlala, accused of attempting to murder actor Tebogo Thobejane, is ongoing at Johannesburg's high court. Matlala, along with his wife Tsakane and three others, faces 25 ch...

item.title: Prince George begins adolescence with Eton College transition
item.description: Prince George, now 13, has officially entered his teenage years with a new birthday portrait released by the royal family. The image captures him smiling at Kensington Palace, taken shortly after Troo...

item.title: Troops Eliminate Boko Haram Photographer, Weaken Propaganda Efforts
item.description: Troops of Operation Hadin KAI (OPHK) have successfully eliminated Abu Salim al-Barnawi, the chief photographer and propaganda operative of Boko Haram/ISWAP, during a failed attack on a military locati...

item.title: Ontario Faces Worst Wildfire Season on Record, Over 180 Fires Threaten Communities
item.description: Ontario is experiencing its worst wildfire season on record, with over 180 active fires burning across the north. Early estimates indicate the flames have scorched more than 7,250 square kilometers of...

item.title: The Debate Over Eric Clapton's Legacy: Perspectives from Renowned Guitarists
item.description: The article explores the differing opinions among prominent guitarists regarding Eric Clapton's status as one of the greatest players in rock history. While many acknowledge his technical mastery and ...

item.title: Senior Wins P56M in PCSO Lotto Draw; No Major Winners on July 22
item.description: A senior citizen from Pila, Laguna, has won a P56 million jackpot in the PCSO Lotto 6/42 draw, marking a significant victory for the lucky bettor. The PCSO confirmed that the winner, who purchased a P...

item.title: Curfew imposed in Mastung's Khud Kocha following security incidents
item.description: Authorities in Mastung's Khud Kocha district have imposed an indefinite curfew following a series of security incidents, including a bus attack and bridge collapse. The attacks resulted in casualties ...

item.title: Parliament defends NSFAS accommodation cap as student leaders push for its removal
item.description: Student representative councils (SRCs) from 10 universities have intensified calls for the National Student Financial Aid Scheme (NSFAS) accommodation cap to be scrapped, citing unsustainable pricing ...

item.title: TIB Rasilimali and Orbit Securities Collaborate to Boost Capital Raising in Tanzania
item.description: TIB Rasilimali, the capital markets subsidiary of state-owned TIB Development Bank, and brokerage Orbit Securities have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to expand capital raising in Tanzania. The ...

item.title: NFL Player from Pickering Charged in Durham Gangs and Guns Investigation
item.description: The article reports that an NFL player from Pickering, Ontario, has been charged as part of a broader Durham Regional Police investigation into firearms and gang-related activities. The individual is ...

item.title: 22 more infant remains found at Tuam mother and baby home site
item.description: The remains of 22 additional infants have been discovered at the former St Mary’s mother and baby home in Tuam, Co Galway, bringing the total number of infant remains found at the site to 99 since exc...

item.title: CNN Anchor Confronts Rep. Buddy Carter Over Trump's War Promises
item.description: CNN anchor Brianna Keilar engaged in a heated exchange with Republican Rep. Buddy Carter during a live segment, questioning his defense of President Donald Trump's military actions in the Middle East....

item.title: Kano State Government Launches Free Mass Wedding Program, Warns Against Fraud
item.description: The Kano State Hisbah Board has issued a warning to the public against paying money to anyone claiming to facilitate registration for the state’s mass wedding programme, calling such demands fraudulen...

item.title: San Miguel Beermen confident in George King's role as import
item.description: The San Miguel Beermen expressed confidence in their new import, George King, as the ideal fit for the team during the PBA Season 50 Governors' Cup. The article highlights the team's belief in King's ...

item.title: U.S. Tariffs on Canadian Goods Spark Trade Tensions and Negotiation Challenges
item.description: The U.S. government has imposed new tariffs on Canadian goods under Section 338 of the Trade Act, citing discriminatory practices by Canada in response to U.S. trade policies. Prime Minister Mark Carn...

item.title: India's Youth Protests Challenge Modi Government Over Education and Unemployment
item.description: Tens of thousands of young protesters have taken to the streets of New Delhi, challenging Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government over systemic issues in India's education system and high youth unem...

item.title: Ukraine's Flamingo missiles redefine battlefield dynamics with long-range precision strikes
item.description: Ukraine's FP-5 Flamingo cruise missiles, powered by repurposed Soviet engines sourced from landfill sites, are revolutionizing the conflict against Russia. Weighing six metric tonnes and capable of fl...

item.title: Woman Arrested for Abducting 7-Month-Old Baby in Laguna
item.description: A 51-year-old woman has been arrested for the alleged abduction of a 7-month-old infant in Laguna, Philippines. The incident, which has sparked public concern, involves a local police investigation in...

item.title: Government Considers Compensation for NEET Leak Victims, Avoids Sacking Pradhan
item.description: The Indian government is unlikely to remove Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan despite mounting demands for his resignation, as it believes he cannot be held responsible for the NEET paper le...

item.title: US 30-Year Bond Yield Hits 5% Threshold, Raising Concerns Over Debt and Inflation
item.description: The US 30-year bond yield has remained above 5% for the longest period since the 2007 financial crisis, sparking concerns about rising debt levels and persistent inflation. According to Bloomberg data...
